zh.lifeSummary: 當Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg於大約1246年11月出生在德國海因里希_普洛文次 布藍登波格時，他的父親Markgraf Otto III. von Brandenburg der Fromme34歲，母親Beatrix von Böhmen21歲。他於1268年10月22日與Judith von Henneberg-Coburg在德國布藍登波格布藍登波格 安 德 哈維爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1298年8月23日在德國海因里希_普洛文次 布藍登波格可萊斯 札-貝爾格萊寧享年51歲，埋葬在德國布藍登波格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg于大约1246年11月出生在德国海因里希_普洛文次 布藍登波格时，他的父亲Markgraf Otto III. von Brandenburg der Fromme34岁，母亲Beatrix von Böhmen21岁。他于1268年10月22日与Judith von Henneberg-Coburg在德国布藍登波格布藍登波格 安 德 哈維爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1298年8月23日在德国海因里希_普洛文次 布藍登波格可萊斯 札-貝爾格萊寧享年51岁，埋葬在德国布藍登波格。

nameMeanings.name: Markgraf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gerhard, Wolf.

German and Dutch: status name from Middle High German marcgrāve ‘margrave’ (which is a title derived from marc ‘boundary’ + grāve ‘royal judge’). In some cases the title may have been applied as a nickname for someone with overbearing behavior or as a metonymic occupational name for a servant in the household of a margrave.

variants.name: Margraf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Mathias.
German and Dutch (of German origin): variant of   Markgraf  .

variants.name: Margrave
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gerhard, Wolf.

German and Dutch: status name from Middle High German marcgrāve ‘margrave’ (which is a title derived from marc ‘boundary’ + grāve ‘royal judge’). In some cases the title may have been applied as a nickname for someone with overbearing behavior or as a metonymic occupational name for a servant in the household of a margrave.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Brandenburg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from Brandenburg, the name of a province, its principal city, and numerous other places.

variants.name: Brandenberg
meanings: German and Swiss German: habitational name from any of several minor places called Brandenberg, for example in Bavaria and Rhineland.
German: in some cases, probably also a variant of   Brandenburg  .

variants.name: Brandenburger
meanings: German: habitational name for someone from Brandenburg (see   Brandenburg  ).

nameMeanings.name: Otto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a short form of any of the various Germanic compound personal names containing the element od, ot ‘prosperity, wealth’ ( compare the corresponding Old English ēad in names such as Edward and Edwin ). St Otto of Bamberg ( d. 1139 ) was a missionary to the Pomeranians. Otto the Great ( 912–73 ) is generally regarded as the founder of the Holy Roman Empire, and the name has been borne by several members of German and Austrian royal houses. It was recorded occasionally among immigrants to England from the Low Countries in the 14th century, but failed to establish itself. Since the 19th century it has again been used occasionally in the English-speaking world, mostly among immigrants from Germanic countries.

en.lifeSummary: When Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg was born about November 1246, in Brandenburg, Prussia, Germany, his father, Markgraf Otto III. von Brandenburg der Fromme, was 34 and his mother, Beatrix von Böhmen, was 21. He married Judith von Henneberg-Coburg on 22 October 1268, in Brandenburg an der Havel, Brandenburg, Germany. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 23 August 1298, in Lehnin, Zauch-Belzig, Brandenburg, Prussia, Germany, at the age of 51, and was buried in Brandenburg, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg est né environ novembre 1246, à Provinz Brandenburg, Prusse, Allemagne, son père, Markgraf Otto III. von Brandenburg der Fromme, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Beatrix von Böhmen, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Judith von Henneberg-Coburg le 22 octobre 1268, à Brandenburg an der Havel, Brandenburg, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 23 août 1298, à Lehnin, Kreis Zauch-Belzig, Provinz Brandenburg, Prusse, Allemagne, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterré à Brandenburg, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg um November 1246 in Provinz Brandenburg, Preußen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Markgraf Otto III. von Brandenburg der Fromme 34 und seine Mutter Beatrix von Böhmen 21. Er hat am 22. Oktober 1268 in Brandenburg an der Havel, Brandenburg, Deutschland Judith von Henneberg-Coburg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 23. August 1298 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Lehnin, Kreis Zauch-Belzig, Provinz Brandenburg, Preußen, Deutschland und wurde in Brandenburg, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg è nato il circa novembre 1246 nel Provinz Brandenburg, Preußen, Germania, il suo padre, Markgraf Otto III. von Brandenburg der Fromme, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Beatrix von Böhmen, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Judith von Henneberg-Coburg il 22 ottobre 1268, nel Brandenburg an der Havel, Brandenburg, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 23 agosto 1298, nel Lehnin, Kreis Zauch-Belzig, Provinz Brandenburg, Preußen, Germania, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolto a Brandenburg, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburgが約1246年11月にドイツプレウssンプロヴィンズ ブランデンブルグで生まれた時，父Markgraf Otto III. von Brandenburg der Frommeは34歳，母Beatrix von Böhmenは21歳であった。1268年10月22日にドイツブランデンブルグブランデンブルグ アン ダー ヘイベルでJudith von Henneberg-Coburgと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1298年8月23日にドイツプレウssンプロヴィンズ ブランデンブルグクレイス ザウチ-ベルズイグレニンで51歳で死去し，ドイツブランデンブルグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg(이)가 대략 1246년 11월 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 브랜덴버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Markgraf Otto III. von Brandenburg der Fromme(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrix von Böhmen(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1268년 10월 22일 독일 브랜덴버그 브랜덴버그 앤 데르 하벨에서 Judith von Henneberg-Coburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1298년 8월 23일 독일 프레우ss 프로빈츠 브랜덴버그 크레이스 자ch-벨지그 레h닌에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 브랜덴버그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg nasceu aproximadamente novembro de 1246, em Provinz Brandenburg, Preußen, Alemanha, seu pai, Markgraf Otto III. von Brandenburg der Fromme, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Beatrix von Böhmen, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Judith von Henneberg-Coburg em 22 de outubro de 1268, em Brandenburg an der Havel, Brandenburg, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de agosto de 1298, em Lehnin, Kreis Zauch-Belzig, Provinz Brandenburg, Preußen, Alemanha, com 51 anos, e foi sepultado em Brandenburg, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg родился около ноябрь 1246 г. в местности провинз брэнденбург, Пруссия, Германия, его отцу по имени Markgraf Otto III. von Brandenburg der Fromme было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrix von Böhmen, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Judith von Henneberg-Coburg 22 октября 1268 г. в местности брэнденбург ан дир хавел, брэнденбург, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 23 августа 1298 г. в местности лэнин, крис зауч-белзиг, провинз брэнденбург, Пруссия, Германия в возрасте 51 год и был похоронен в местности брэнденбург, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Otto V. Markgraf von Brandenburg nació aproximadamente noviembre de 1246, en Provinz Brandenburg, Preußen, Alemania, su papá, Markgraf Otto III. von Brandenburg der Fromme, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Beatrix von Böhmen, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Judith von Henneberg-Coburg el 22 de octubre de 1268, en Brandenburg an der Havel, Brandenburg, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 23 de agosto de 1298, en Lehnin, Kreis Zauch-Belzig, Provinz Brandenburg, Preußen, Alemania, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultado en Brandenburg, Alemania.
